title: OpenClaw Benchmark QA
description: Unified QA schema for benchmarks/<name>/qa.jsonl. One QA per line. CI policy: every benchmark must call the `main` agent; sub-agent routing goes through `target_agent` (resolved at runtime by main via sessions_spawn).

qa_id.description: Stable identifier, unique within a benchmark. e.g. QA-001, seed-001, Q1.

agent.description: CI policy: MUST be the string `main`. Sub-agents are reached indirectly via `target_agent` + main's `sessions_spawn`.

target_agent.description: Optional. If present, main delegates the task to this sub-agent via sessions_spawn instead of solving it itself. Allowed values come from openclaw.json -> agents.list[].

skill.description: Optional capability or skill tag. Maps to SOUL.md sections or main agent skills.

task_type.description: Free-form tag for grouping (paper-only, fact-recall, cross-paper, ...).

input_material.description: Either inline string or {path, content} for fixture files. Passed to the agent as input.

question.description: The actual prompt sent to the main agent (after the [BENCHMARK DIRECTIVE] wrapper is applied if `target_agent` is set).

expected_artifacts.description: Glob paths under the agent workspace that should exist after the run (e.g. wiki/domains/foo/papers/bar.md).

gold_answer.description: Reference answer. Used by rule-based judge.

rubric.description: Free-form LLM-judge prompt. Concatenated with the agent output when calling judge_with_agent.

rubric_dimensions.description: Optional list of dimensions. 0-2 score per dimension is the default.

pass_threshold.description: Average rubric score (0-1) above which the QA is considered passed.

weight.description: Weight in benchmark average. Set <1 for low-priority smoke cases.

judge.description: rules = judge_with_rules; agent = judge_with_agent (LLM judge through dedicated reviewer agent); skip = only record raw output.
